what_it_does: A small React (Babel-in-browser) component file defining two global components: ConceptVIcon, which renders an icon SVG by looking up its name in window.CONCEPTV_ICONS and injecting the path body via dangerouslySetInnerHTML; and ConceptVBadge, a gold-circle wrapper that places a ConceptVIcon inside a bordered round badge. Both are attached to window.
observations: File header marks itself DEPRECATED — directs new work to CvIcon (CvIcon.jsx + cv-icons.js), 'the single live icon home'.
Reads from window.CONCEPTV_ICONS (legacy icon-paths.js), a DIFFERENT registry than cv-icons.js's window.CV_ICONS.
Unknown icon name renders a visible dashed-square placeholder with the missing name as text (fail-visible, not silent).
Supports a 'bg' prop that string-replaces var(--cv-icon-bg, #fff) in the icon body and sets the CSS var on the wrapper.
Props: name, size=20, color='currentColor', weight=1.5, bg, style, plus rest-spread onto the svg.
ConceptVBadge defaults: size 56, gold color, gold-faint background, inner icon ~50% of badge size.
Uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML to inject raw SVG path strings.
standouts: Explicit DEPRECATED banner pointing to the single live successor (CvIcon / cv-icons.js).
Missing-icon placeholder makes absent icons visible rather than blank — a fail-loud rendering choice.
Reads the legacy window.CONCEPTV_ICONS registry, distinct from the CV_ICONS registry the rest of the set uses.
conventions: Attaches components to window (script-tag / Babel load model, no ES modules).
24x24 viewBox, fill=none, stroke=currentColor, rounded caps/joins — matches the system's icon drawing convention.
Follows the design system's 'one live home' rule by explicitly deprecating itself toward CvIcon.
Uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML, acceptable here because bodies are trusted internal SVG strings.
concerns: Kept only for back-compat against a legacy registry (window.CONCEPTV_ICONS) that differs from the active window.CV_ICONS — two icon-data sources coexist.
String .replace of the bg token only matches the literal 'var(--cv-icon-bg, ?#fff)' pattern; bodies using a different fallback color would not be substituted.
notes: A deprecated legacy React icon/badge renderer over window.CONCEPTV_ICONS, kept for back-compat; new code should use CvIcon instead.
questions: Is window.CONCEPTV_ICONS (icon-paths.js) still populated, or fully superseded by CV_ICONS?
Are there remaining call sites of ConceptVIcon/ConceptVBadge, or is this dead-but-retained?
Does CvIcon offer the same gold-circle badge convenience, or is ConceptVBadge's pattern reimplemented there?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header states it is a deprecated back-compat renderer for the legacy icon list, and that is exactly what the code is.
apparent_themes: Back-compat preservation during a registry migration
intention_signals: Trying to keep older usages working during the migration to the consolidated CvIcon/CV_ICONS icon home, while clearly signposting that this is the old path and steering new work elsewhere.
contribution: Contributes the legacy icon/badge render path (over window.CONCEPTV_ICONS) and the gold-circle entity-badge wrapper pattern, plus the explicit deprecation pointer to the successor CvIcon.
summary_for_embedding: A deprecated React/Babel icon component (ConceptVIcon) and gold-circle badge (ConceptVBadge) that render SVG icons from the legacy window.CONCEPTV_ICONS registry, kept for back-compat and pointing new work to CvIcon.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve older icon-rendering call sites against the legacy icon-paths registry during migration to the consolidated CvIcon/CV_ICONS single source.
